sessions.lever: random permuter (7188 iter, base score 20) + 1 additional structural variant (typed-ptr subscript `p[v*8]` w/ outer pointer assignment)
sessions.summary: Set up directed permuter workspace at permuter/func_80078B04/ (base.c=candidate; target.s from asm/funcs/func_80078B04.s + permuter prelude). 4-thread random permuter ran 7188 iterations, never broke base score 20 (weighted score for the 2-insn diff: addu reg encoding + lhu base). Confirmed: also tried typed-pointer subscript form `u16 *p = (u16 *)D_8009BD6C; result = p[v*8];` — regressed to score 7 (same regression family as shift-first variants). Random permuter + 74 manual variants + new structural variant: random-mutation surface is exhausted. Floor 2 plateau is real. The remaining 2-insn diff requires either (a) a directed-PERM permuter probe with explicit axis macros that random-only can't hit, OR (b) a non-randomization-discoverable C-shape that flips RTL `(plus a b)` operand order without disturbing the named-intermediate's allocno preference.

sessions.lever: directed permuter PERM_GENERAL on the address expression (4 alternatives: lw+sll, sll+lw, (v<<4)+lw, lw+(v<<4)) + bonus alternatives on the mask form and the comparison
sessions.summary: Re-measured candidate floor at f99cc056: sandbox=2, build_insns=14, target_insns=14 (matches prior). Set up directed PERM_GENERAL macros covering: address-expr (`D_8009BD6C+v*0x10`, `v*0x10+D_8009BD6C`, `(v<<4)+D_8009BD6C`, `D_8009BD6C+(v<<4)`); mask form (`arg0 & 0xFFFF`, `(s32)(u16)arg0`); compare form (`v<3`, `(u32)v<3U`, `v<=2`). Ran 4-thread permuter 240s. Base score 25 (HIGHER than session-3's 20 because the comparison alternative `(u32)v<3U` adds a typing instruction in some paths). Best iter score: 25 (no improvement). Random surface re-confirmed exhausted; directed permutation across the explicit address-order axis still does NOT close the gap. Mechanism re-confirmed: every shift-first variant Random mutates toward regresses to 100-1200+ score because it breaks the named-intermediate's pseudo allocation preference (the `u16 *p` named intermediate is the floor-7->2 lever); reverting to non-named regresses to 7.

prior_sessions_summary: Session 1 (2026-06-12, head fe15e792): u16 *p named intermediate (split addr expr from deref). Floor 7->2. Reviewer PASS.

Session 2 (2026-06-12, head fe15e792): consolidation sweep — 38 manual variants (pointer-arith spellings, named-intermediate placements, structural rearrangements). RTL analysis from i.lreg + i.greg dumps confirmed: pseudo 74 (addu output) takes $v0 because its FIRST source (pseudo 79, lw dest) is in $v0; reversing to (plus 78 79) requires reversing C operand order, which regresses by killing named-intermediate preference. Floor stayed 2.

reviewer.evidence: u16 *p is a live named intermediate (assigned + dereferenced); analogous to SOTN-sanctioned named-intermediate-declaration-order family (no-new-park-categories.md SOTN-accepted). Not a forbidden cheat family; passes all 6 checklist tests. Candidate unchanged across sessions 1-4 — verdict carries forward.

remaining_gap.summary: Coupled allocator tiebreak. Insn 22 in i.greg: `(set 74 (plus 79 78))` where 79=lw(D_8009BD6C)->$v0, 78=sll(v,4)->$v1. Output 74 takes $v0 because (a) it's the plus's first source's register and (b) downstream (lhu base, then result return) all want $v0. Target needs `(set 78 (plus 78 79))` so 78 (now also addu dest) stays in $v1, freeing the lhu's dest from base==dest collapse. Reversing the plus order requires reversing the C expression order, but every shift-first form (v40 ofs+D_..., v45 ofs+=, v50 sv+=, v64 m2c-form, v65 m2c-form-p, typed-ptr-subscript, directed-PERM session-4 alternatives) breaks either the named-intermediate's preference push OR the trampoline-shape (collapses to 11-13 build_insns). Random + directed permuter both exhausted on the candidate's structural surface.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD has $2 <-> $3 @3-6 + subst $4->$3 @0,1,3 + lhu $2,0($2)->$2,0($3) @7 — 5 rules. Candidate would still need 2 substs: addu output rename (74->v1) + lhu base rewrite.

next_hypotheses: GCC source dive — `tools/gcc-2.7.2/expr.c` (`expand_expr`, `simplify_binary_operation`) and `combine.c` (`simplify_logical`, `simplify_comparison`). Locate the canonicalization rule for commutative PLUS during RTL expansion. If pseudo number ordering or operand 'simpler' classification controls the swap, identify a C-shape that creates the sll-feeding pseudo at a lower number than the lw-feeding pseudo. Specifically read mips.c's `mips_legitimize_address` for any plus-canonicalization on the target side.

Sibling cross-ref via instrumented dumps — `BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG=1 BB2_PRIO_DEBUG=1 tmp/gccdbg/cc1` on BOTH the candidate and a synthesized standalone of func_80078A68's body (sibling that matched with `(t0 * 0x10) + D_8009BD6C` shift-first), diff the allocno-preference and conflict-graph dumps at the addu insn. A68 is a STORE-base-reuse pattern, but its addu lands sll-first naturally — what shape of the input C drove that?

Directed PERM with PERM_RANDOMIZE + PERM_LINESWAP on the if-body — random mode only mutates AST locally; PERM_RANDOMIZE forces line-reorder permutations the random walk doesn't reach. Combine with explicit named-intermediate placement axes (PERM_GENERAL across [u16 *p, s32 *p, u32 q, void *p]) to test whether the typed pointer family has a higher-numbered allocno preference shape that flips the plus's input order without breaking the trampoline.

rejected_forms.reason: Improves direction (in-place addu) but mask still lives in $a0 because v is the accumulator and GCC keeps the original arg's register. Not a cheat — just doesn't reach 2.
Hoists the pointer load OUT of the if-true branch — target's lw is INSIDE the if. Structural mismatch raises distance.

rejected_forms.form: register T x asm("$3") pin or single-instruction __asm__ to force $v1
rejected_forms.reason: register-asm pin + hardcoded-$N asm injection — forbidden by inline-asm-policy.md and inline-asm-injection.md.

rejected_forms.form: shift-first expression order family (v40 ofs+D_..., v45 ofs+=, v50 sv+=, v46 ofs+D_cast, v64/v65 m2c early-return forms, p[v*8] typed-ptr subscript w/ outer base assign, session-4 directed-PERM 4-way address-expr alternatives)
rejected_forms.reason: Reversing the C operand to put the shift on the LEFT regresses the score. Either kills the named-intermediate preference (back to score 7) or breaks the if/else trampoline structure (collapses build_insns to 11-13, score 8-12). Session 4 directed-PERM on this axis: base score 25 (higher than the candidate's 20) because the alternative compare/mask forms introduce extra typing insns; no iter broke into score 0.

rejected_forms.form: named-intermediate variants holding floor 2 (v37 ofs+p, v39 byteptr, v41 addr_arith, v42 sv_intermediate, v43 pluseq_byte, v44 struct_alias, v47 base_idx, v48 base_idx_inline, v49 sv_then_p, v52 u32 addr cast, v54 v_extends, v55 struct.w arrow, v57-60 various, v68 uint_p, v69-74 various)
rejected_forms.reason: All preserve the candidate's named-intermediate allocno-preference push (mask->$v1) but cannot reverse the plus's RTL operand order. Confirms the floor-2 plateau is a real coupled tiebreak, not a lever-search shallowness issue. ~38 variants across this session, ~36 in prior session = ~74 explored forms.
Mild regression vs s32 v — the unsigned mask interaction with slt nudges the comparison RTL. No closing path.

rejected_forms.form: Random permuter (7188 iter session 3, base 20 = floor 2; + session 4 directed-PERM ~36 iter, base 25, no break)
rejected_forms.reason: Random-mutation surface around the candidate is exhausted. Directed PERM_GENERAL across address-expression order axis (4 alternatives) also exhausted; the 4-alternative explicit form raised the base score by introducing extra typing insns in the (u32)v<3U compare alternative. Closing form, if it exists, requires either (a) GCC-source-derived C-shape that the explicit alternatives didn't enumerate, OR (b) instrumented dump comparison vs A68 to identify the specific allocno-priority lever.
